domain: Hawaiian fishpond loko iʻa kuapā with mākāhā
specific_mechanism: Size-selective grate gate that closes as cohort matures; passive tidal transport.
llm_application: MAKAHA: pretraining data gate with monotone-closing token-count + complexity threshold at fixed checkpoint cadence — small simple examples admitted early; threshold tightens; only complex examples admitted later. Previously-admitted examples continue contributing.
novelty_claim: DataFlow (2512.16676) does filter-stack at preparation; multilingual filtering (2505.22232) is quality-based; standard curriculum learning increases difficulty. MAKAHA's specific mechanism — monotone CLOSING gate at tide-cadence checkpoints + retention of prior — is not yet a published primitive.
